item.text: I’ll inspect the repository instructions and existing files, derive the graph/DP solution, then implement and test `solution.py` with samples and small brute-force checks.

The functional graph reduces cleanly: every directed cycle must have one common value, and after contracting each cycle the remaining edges form rooted trees whose values are nondecreasing toward the root. I’m implementing an iterative \(O(NM)\) DP so long chains cannot hit Python’s recursion limit.

item.text: The implementation matches all three samples. I’m now checking it against exhaustive enumeration on many small random functional graphs, then I’ll run a maximum-size performance case.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import itertools
import random
import subprocess

random.seed(387)
for case in range(120):
    n = random.randint(1, 7)
    m = random.randint(1, 4)
    a = [random.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
    expected = 0
    for x in itertools.product(range(1, m + 1), repeat=n):
        if all(x[i] <= x[a[i]] for i in range(n)):
            expected += 1
    data = f\"{n} {m}\\n\" + \" \".join(str(v + 1) for v in a) + \"\\n\"
    actual = int(subprocess.check_output([\"python3\", \"solution.py\"], input=data.encode()))
    if actual "'!= expected:
        raise SystemExit((case, n, m, a, expected, actual))
print("120 randomized brute-force cases passed")
PY'

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import itertools
import random
import subprocess

random.seed(387)
for case in range(120):
    n = random.randint(1, 7)
    m = random.randint(1, 4)
    a = [random.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
    expected = 0
    for x in itertools.product(range(1, m + 1), repeat=n):
        if all(x[i] <= x[a[i]] for i in range(n)):
            expected += 1
    data = f\"{n} {m}\\n\" + \" \".join(str(v + 1) for v in a) + \"\\n\"
    actual = int(subprocess.check_output([\"python3\", \"solution.py\"], input=data.encode()))
    if actual "'!= expected:
        raise SystemExit((case, n, m, a, expected, actual))
print("120 randomized brute-force cases passed")
PY'
